description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly convex sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound by two dark metal hoops at the top and bottom. The wood grain is clearly visible, giving it a rustic and aged appearance, and there are some visible stains and markings on the side, including a patch of discoloration and a faint inscription. The barrel has a large open mouth at one end, revealing a hollow interior, and the opposite end is fully closed with a flat wooden base. There is a small, rough handle or remnant of a handle attached near the open end, likely for carrying or tilting the barrel. The surface shows signs of wear, with some chipped paint and minor abrasions, especially around the rim and base. This object would typically be found in urban settings such as courtyards, garages, or near workshops, serving as a storage or utility container.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with metal hoops, an open mouth, and visible wood grain, showing signs of wear and urban use.
description_view_0: The open mouth of the barrel is visible, showing the hollow interior and the thickness of the wooden staves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved wooden body, metal hoops, and the closed wooden base.
description_view_2: Top-down view showing the full length of the barrel, metal hoops, and visible stains and markings on the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the closed wooden base and the attached handle remnant.
